EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS TO THE DECLARATION OF SAN RIVINIUS IN OPPOSITION TO DEFT'S MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE AND MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION FILED BY DEFENDANT AMERICAN EMPLOYER GROUP APPLIED UNDERWRITERS

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANTS AMERICAN EMPLOYER GROUP AND APPLIED UNDERWRITERS' MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION & STAY PROCEEDINGS IS DENIED. KARMAZIN'S DECLARATION IS INADEQUATE PROOF OF MAILING OF REVISED TERMS. IN ANY EVENT, TO THE EXTENT ANY SERVICE AGREEMENT IS INCORPORATED IT IS THE 2000 SERVICE AGREEMENT AND SIGNED AUTHORIZATIONS ARE NOT SUFFICIENT TO MODIFY THAT AGREEMENT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

MOTION To Compel Arbitration & Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Sep-28-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael K. Perkins of Fine, Boggs & Perkins, LLP, 650-712-8908, appearing for defendants and moving parties
Matthew R. Troughton of Sheppard Mullin Richter & Hampton, LLP, 434-9100, appearing for plaintiff and responding party

This matter was heard on September 28, 2006. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS AMERICAN EMPLOYER GROUP and APPLIED UNDERWRITERS' MOTION To Compel Arbitration & Stay Proceedings is denied. Karmazin's declaration is inadequate proof of mailing of revised terms. In any event, to the extent any service agreement is incorporated it is the 2000 service agreement and signed authorizations are not sufficient to modify that agreement. Prevailing party to prepare form of order. (301/PJB/xKB) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 8189

MOTION To Change Venue Purs To The Parties' Contractual Forum-Selection Clause
Off Calendar Sep-28-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael K. Perkins of Fine, Boggs & Perkins, LLP, 650-712-8908, appearing for defendants and moving parties
Matthew R. Troughton of Sheppard Mullin Richter & Hampton, LLP, 434-9100, appearing for plaintiff and responding party

This matter was heard on September 28, 2006. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS AMERICAN EMPLOYER GROUP and APPLIED UNDERWRITERS' MOTION To Change Venue Pursuant To The Parties' Contractual Forum-Selection Clause is denied. Karmazin's declaration is inadequate proof of mailing of revised terms. In any event, to the extent any service agreement is incorporated it is the 2000 service agreement and signed authorizations are not sufficient to modify that agreement. Prevailing party to prepare form of order. (301/PJB/xKB) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 8189
